name: The Union

street_level_faction_presence.street_signals: Symbols, uniform cues, work marks, and rumor shorthand make power visible in daily movement.

greetings: Introductions often include trade function, guild tie, or route purpose before personal warmth.
hospitality_rules: Hospitality is transactional but not cold: food, bench space, and negotiation follow quickly together.
insult_triggers: failing a bargain in bad faith
taboo_topics: casual claims over Mal succession rights without standing
class_expectations: value is measured by usefulness, brokerage, or production clarity more than bloodline alone
faith_etiquette: faith appears in practical shrines and oath language more than grand devotional display
suspicious_behavior: appearing at freight yards without affiliation or purpose
outsider_mistakes: assuming neutral mediation means lack of politics

occupation_and_daily_life.daily_rhythm: Union life is crowded with arbitration, freight movement, guild disputes, workshops, changing obligations, and public notice culture.
occupation_and_daily_life.work_rhythm_by_calendar_or_season: Market days, sanction windows, and route availability shape time more than noble ceremony does.
occupation_and_daily_life.ordinary_texture: The air is full of chalk dust, ink, rope, cooking oil, hot metal, wet crates, and argument. Streets feel loud, useful, and perpetually provisional.

travel_and_transition_texture: Union travel is a chain of handoffs, stamps, witnesses, route negotiations, and people trying to turn delay into leverage.
dwelling_and_settlement_life: Housing ranges from bunk-heavy guild rows to merchant houses with ledger rooms and visitor benches near entry doors.
relationship_and_reputation_logic: People are remembered for speed, fairness, payment discipline, and whether they break networks when strained.
region_specific_emotional_tone: Union stories are sharp, social, crowded, and opportunistic: reputations rise or collapse fast, and every agreement has witnesses, resentment, or consequence.
